content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you a string SSS of length NNN, then he asked you whether it is possible to rearrange the characters such that no character appears thrice consecutively.

If it is possible then print **YES** otherwise print **No**.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the string.
The second line of every test case contains a string SSS of size NNN.


Output Format
For each testcase, output whether it is possible to rearrange the characters such that no character appears thrice consecutively.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the string. The second line contains a string S of length N consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to rearrange the characters of S so that no character appears three times consecutively, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "wsww" already satisfies the condition (no three identical letters together).\nTest case 2: The string consists of three 'w's only, so any arrangement will have three consecutive 'w's.\nTest case 3: A possible rearrangement is "xwakwms" itself, which has no three identical letters consecutively.

solution.approach: For each test case count the frequency of every character. Let c_max be the maximum frequency and let rest = N - c_max be the total number of all other characters. To avoid three identical letters in a row we can place at most two copies of the most frequent character together, forming ceil(c_max/2) groups. Between any two groups we need at least one different character, therefore we need rest >= ceil(c_max/2) - 1. This condition is equivalent to rest >= floor((c_max - 1) / 2). If it holds, a valid rearrangement exists (constructible by a simple greedy placement), otherwise it is impossible. The algorithm runs in O(N) per test case for counting frequencies.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the string. Overall O(∑N) for all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (26 counters for lowercase letters).
